title: Couldnt Ramiel just blast his way into the Geofront
display_title: Couldnt Ramiel just blast his way into the Geofront
wikitext:
[]: Couldn't Ramiel just blast his way into the Geofront?             Quote Post by EVA X » Wed Jan 13, 2010 4:50 pm I mean, why not? His strongest attack blew away an entire mountain. That's gotta account for something when going against the Geofront.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Jan 13, 2010 5:07 pm And risk killing Lillith? But even if he could avoid that I don't think Ramiel could just shoot straight down.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Wed Jan 13, 2010 5:09 pm It couldn't shoot down for some reason involving the Earth's magnetic field, gravitational flux, and high concentrations of plotanium in the city's armour.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Jan 13, 2010 5:21 pm And plot. Don't forget plot.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Wed Jan 13, 2010 5:37 pm If ramiel shot straight down,we wouldnt be able to see it's badass way of shapeshifiting.And that alone made him/her my favorite angel.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA X » Wed Jan 13, 2010 5:37 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: And risk killing Lillith? But even if he could avoid that I don't think Ramiel could just shoot straight down.

[]:             Quote Post by Armitage » Wed Jan 13, 2010 7:25 pm I think it also depends on the personality of the angel which we sometimes forget they possess . (some spoilers follow) My guess , actually , is that Ramiel is quite vulnerable despite being a flying fortress . We know that in order to active one of his abilities he needs to expose his core (hence why Misato carries out the seemingly futile attacks so that unit 01 can snipe) . If Ramiel were to power up , charge , and burn through the ground , he might be vulnerable to a direct attack either by 01 or something else . Plus , all of this speculation is assuming that the angels know what's in the geofront . I actually find it interesting actually that it's not until after ramiel that sahaquiel and ramiel start tearing up NERV hq . My theory is that angel knowledge might be passed on from one incarnation to the next (not sure how kaworu fits into all that) and hence once ramiel breached the geofront , the angels realized what kind of fortification they're dealing with . Hence why they later took on more direct and destructive views towards tokyo-3 .

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Wed Jan 13, 2010 9:40 pm Jesus Christ some of you people can squeeze a river from a rock. There isn't even an in-universe explanation for half the dumb shit that happens in *this* universe.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA X » Wed Jan 13, 2010 10:04 pm Armitage wrote: I think it also depends on the personality of the angel which we sometimes forget they possess . (some spoilers follow) My guess , actually , is that Ramiel is quite vulnerable despite being a flying fortress . We know that in order to active one of his abilities he needs to expose his core (hence why Misato carries out the seemingly futile attacks so that unit 01 can snipe) . If Ramiel were to power up , charge , and burn through the ground , he might be vulnerable to a direct attack either by 01 or something else . Plus , all of this speculation is assuming that the angels know what's in the geofront . I actually find it interesting actually that it's not until after ramiel that sahaquiel and ramiel start tearing up NERV hq . My theory is that angel knowledge might be passed on from one incarnation to the next (not sure how kaworu fits into all that) and hence once ramiel breached the geofront , the angels realized what kind of fortification they're dealing with . Hence why they later took on more direct and destructive views towards tokyo-3 .

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Jan 14, 2010 2:22 pm Did you forget Sachiel blasts through all the levels of the geofront eventually as well? They just vary in power, there's no evidence to support knowledge being passed down. Hell, with few exceptions, the angels just run on the instinct to return to what they think is their mommy.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 14, 2010 3:19 pm Well, in theory, the souls of dead Angels could keep floating around the Tokyo-3 vicinity and whisper Protips to their siblings. EVA X: Please title your threads descriptively. Half-sentences don't easily convey the subject of the thread, if at all. Dartz wrote: There isn't even an in-universe explanation for half the dumb shit that happens in *this* universe.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA X » Thu Jan 14, 2010 6:29 pm Consider it done. Did you forget Sachiel blasts through all the levels of the geofront eventually as well? They just vary in power, there's no evidence to support knowledge being passed down. Hell, with few exceptions, the angels just run on the instinct to return to what they think is their mommy.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jan 14, 2010 8:37 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: And risk killing Lillith? But even if he could avoid that I don't think Ramiel could just shoot straight down.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jan 15, 2010 7:23 am Seeing as how the Angel shot straight through the hills as if they were butter, it would be a very quick efficient way to destroy the very Lilith it was after. Yet another thematic element within Evangelion: Too much power is a bad thing, and causes you to destroy the very things you love.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 15, 2010 9:04 am This very same question can be asked for the series Ramiel; both of them share the same answer: plot. Had Ramiel shot straight down, IT'D HAVE BEEN GAME OVER, MAN! GAME OVER!

[]:             Quote Post by Mad » Sat Jan 16, 2010 12:28 pm Axell wrote: Did you forget Sachiel blasts through all the levels of the geofront eventually as well? They just vary in power, there's no evidence to support knowledge being passed down. Hell, with few exceptions, the angels just run on the instinct to return to what they think is their mommy.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat Jan 16, 2010 3:41 pm Dartz wrote: plotanium in the city's armour.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 16, 2010 4:56 pm Mad wrote: Didn't Sachiel cheat and hit a building? I'm pretty sure he blasts a building which effectively knocks it down into the Geofront.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat Jan 16, 2010 8:21 pm Sachi wrote: Then he blasts again and we see the cross explosion coming from the ceiling of the Geofront, showing that he made it through all the way.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Jan 16, 2010 8:34 pm He attacks more than once.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Jan 13, 2010 5:07 pm And risk killing Lillith? But even if he could avoid that
